title: Planetary Gear
goal: Shows compact orbital gear motion where a sun gear drives one or more planet gears on a carrier.
gallery_summary: Compact sun-and-planet gear motion with a carrier/output handle.
parts: Sun Gear: Central gear pinned to the board
Planet Gear: Orbiting gear mounted on the carrier
Carrier Link: Holds planet axle and output handle hole
Ring Guide: Visual reference for the planet orbit
Spacer Stack: Separates board, carrier, and gears
advantages: Produces compact geared motion in a small board area
Demonstrates ratio, carrier, and orbital ideas clearly
Can be prototyped with the same small gear and linkage parts
Supports multiple planets for balanced visual motion
disadvantages: More sensitive to axle alignment than a two-gear train
Full ring-gear fabrication is more advanced than the starter board demo
Extra planets add assembly complexity and friction
materials: Paper fasteners for sun, carrier, and planet axle
cautions: Prototype one planet first before adding more planets
Keep the sun fastener straight and the carrier loose
Confirm the planet clears adjacent board holes and brackets
